corrections.description: Replaced stale pilot data (0.05% recovery, d=-0.91) with corrected FULL-mode cross-domain patching results
corrections.old_claim: mechanism does not generalize to semantic hierarchies, revealing a divide between concentrated and distributed absorption
corrections.new_claim: competitive exclusion confirmed across all tested hierarchies: first-letter d=1.33, city-continent d=1.50, city-language d=0.75

corrections.description: Updated second key finding from 'fails for semantic hierarchies' to 'confirms across all hierarchies'
corrections.old_claim: city-continent child zeroing recovers only 0.05% (control: 14.5%), effect reversed
corrections.new_claim: city-continent 61.9% recovery (control 5.2%, d=1.50), city-language 34.2% recovery (control 6.8%, d=0.75)

corrections.description: Reframed '100% pathological' to 'child decoder vectors carry large-magnitude parent information' with circularity acknowledgment
corrections.old_claim: Absorption is 100% pathological. The hypothesis that absorption might faithfully represent computational redundancy is decisively falsified.
corrections.new_claim: Child decoder vectors carry large-magnitude parent information (3.98 vs 0.004 nats). Diagnostic shares direction with probe (circularity noted).
corrections.rationale: The benign/pathological diagnostic uses the same probe direction for both FN classification and ablation target, creating inherent circularity

corrections.description: Added contribution-based child feature identification method and corrected entity/instance counts
corrections.details: contribution_i = mean_activation_i * cos(d_i, w_p); features with most negative contribution identified as absorbers

corrections.description: Added circularity note to benign vs pathological diagnostic methodology
corrections.details: Explicit acknowledgment that parent direction w_p is from the same probe used for FN classification. Suggested genuine test requirements: independent readout, held-out probe, downstream task accuracy.

corrections.description: Updated Figure 4 caption with corrected three-hierarchy patching data
Replaced 'Cross-Domain Patching Fails (City-Continent)' with 'Cross-Domain Patching Confirms Universal Mechanism'
key_changes: Replaced concentrated-vs-distributed dichotomy with universal mechanism finding
Added per-class recovery details for city-continent and city-language
Added cross-hierarchy comparison showing recovery scales with class count
Added methodological note explaining pilot bug and correction

corrections.description: Renamed 'Absorption Is Always Pathological' to 'Decoder Information Entanglement' with full circularity discussion
key_changes: Updated figure caption to note circularity
Acknowledged that genuine test requires independent readout

corrections.description: Updated causal methods discussion from 'partially succeed' to 'succeed' with all three hierarchy effect sizes
corrections.old_title: Correlational Methods Fail; Causal Methods Partially Succeed
corrections.new_title: Correlational Methods Fail; Causal Methods Succeed

corrections.description: Replaced 'Concentrated vs. Distributed Absorption Mechanisms' with 'Universal Competitive Exclusion with Hierarchy-Dependent Recovery'
corrections.new_title: Universal Competitive Exclusion with Hierarchy-Dependent Recovery

corrections.description: Updated safety monitoring implication from '100% pathological impact' to causally confirmed competitive exclusion framing
Updated conclusion contribution 2 with corrected universal causal mechanism
corrections.old_claim: The same intervention fails for city-continent (d=-0.91), revealing concentrated vs distributed divide
corrections.new_claim: Universal competitive exclusion: first-letter d=1.33, city-continent d=1.50, city-language d=0.75, all p<1e-17

corrections.description: Reframed contribution 3 from '100% pathological' to 'large-magnitude parent information in child decoders' with circularity note
Updated limitations: replaced 'causal evidence limited to first-letter' with circularity caveat on decoder diagnostic
Updated future directions from 'concentrated-vs-distributed divide' to independent pathological diagnostics and probe improvement
Architecture claim reframed: added 'In our limited architecture comparison' prefix and 'though this null result may reflect low statistical power rather than true equivalence' qualifier
corrections.old_claim: SAE architecture has no significant effect on absorption rates
corrections.new_claim: In our limited architecture comparison, SAE architecture shows no significant effect... though this null result may reflect low statistical power

corrections.description: Qualified the 4x range as descriptive: added within-RAVEL statistical significance with Bonferroni-corrected pairwise details; noted only city-language reaches significance vs first-letter
corrections.new_claim: Within-RAVEL Kruskal-Wallis p=7.4e-66, N=3545, all pairwise significant after Bonferroni; against first-letter only city-language reaches significance (p_Bonf=0.003)

corrections.description: Added 'underpowered' qualifier to architecture comparison in deployment discussion
corrections.old_claim: hierarchy type, not SAE architecture, determines absorption severity
corrections.new_claim: hierarchy type is a stronger predictor of absorption severity than SAE architecture... though the architecture comparison is underpowered

corrections.description: Reframed architecture section: 'In our limited comparison' prefix, added k=4 and k=3 group counts, acknowledged low statistical power and confounded widths throughout
corrections.old_claim: SAE architecture has no significant effect on absorption rates... This is a negative-as-positive finding
corrections.new_claim: In our limited comparison... This null result should be interpreted cautiously: with only 3-4 architecture configurations and confounded dictionary widths, statistical power is limited

corrections.description: Added circularity cross-reference to decoder entanglement claim in safety discussion; added single-model caveat
corrections.details: Added 'circularity caveat in Section ref{sec:mechanism}' and 'This range is measured on a single model (Gemma 2 2B) and rates on other architectures may differ'

corrections.description: Qualified 4x range as 'descriptive' in conclusion; added pairwise significance details
corrections.old_claim: Absorption rates span a 4x range at layer 24: 11.6-45.1%
corrections.new_claim: Absorption rates span a descriptive 4x range; within RAVEL all pairwise significant after Bonferroni; against first-letter only city-language significant

corrections.description: Reframed contribution 4 from 'Hierarchy dominates architecture' to 'Hierarchy is a stronger predictor than architecture' with explicit power caveat
corrections.old_claim: SAE architecture has no significant effect
corrections.new_claim: no significant effect... This null result may reflect low statistical power rather than true architecture invariance

corrections.description: Expanded from 4 to 5 limitations: added underpowered architecture comparison and limited pairwise significance between first-letter and RAVEL
corrections.new_claim: Five limitations including: architecture comparison underpowered (3-4 configs with confounded widths); pairwise first-letter vs RAVEL limited by small first-letter sample (n=21)

corrections.description: Qualified 4x variation as 'descriptive' in Section 4 opening; separated within-RAVEL statistical significance from descriptive range claim
corrections.details: Changed '4x variation (Kruskal-Wallis...)' to 'descriptive 4x variation. Within three RAVEL hierarchies, variation is statistically significant (KW...)'

corrections.description: Updated teaser figure caption: qualified 4x as descriptive, clarified within-RAVEL scope, noted only city-language vs first-letter significant
corrections.details: Added '(descriptive range)' and 'Within-RAVEL Kruskal-Wallis... against first-letter, only city-language is significant after Bonferroni correction'

corrections.description: Updated architecture figure caption: added group counts (k=4, k=3) and power limitation note
corrections.details: Added 'k=4' and 'k=3' after p-values and 'The architecture null result may reflect limited statistical power (3-4 configurations with confounded widths)'

corrections.description: Updated hypothesis verdict table caption to reflect current status of all hypotheses including reframed H8 and inconclusive H6
corrections.old_claim: Two hypotheses (H1, H3) are strongly supported; two (H2', H8) are refuted/falsified
corrections.new_claim: H1, H3, H7 are strongly supported. H2' refuted. H8 supported with circularity caveat. H4, H5, H9 fail. H6 inconclusive due to limited power

stale_data_grep.pattern_searched: d=-0.91|0.05% recovery|fails entirely|does not generalize|100% pathological|always pathological
allowed_in_methodological_notes: d=-0.91 appears in 2 methodological notes explaining the correction (lines 268 and 353)
stale_data_grep.concentrated_distributed_check: Appears only in 2 methodological notes (lines 268, 353) explaining the pilot bug correction -- appropriate

qualifier_terms_added.locations: Abstract, Introduction (3 findings + deployment), Section 4 opening, Figure captions (1, 6), Section 6 (architecture), Discussion (6.5 safety), Conclusion (contributions 1, 4, limitations)

summary: 27 corrections applied across abstract, introduction, methodology, mechanism analysis, architecture, discussion, and conclusion. CRITICAL issues resolved: (1) Stale pilot data (d=-0.91) replaced with FULL-mode cross-domain patching (universal mechanism); verified 0 hits for stale patterns. (2) Benign/pathological circularity explicitly acknowledged throughout (12 circularity references). (3) Headline claims qualified: '4x range' marked as descriptive with pairwise significance details; 'no architecture effect' reframed as underpowered null result; Kruskal-Wallis scope clarified as 3 RAVEL hierarchies (N=3,545). (4) Descriptive vs inferential claims separated throughout. (5) Limitations expanded from 4 to 5.
